intro: Mariscus tristis, also known as Marsh Cattail or Yellow Flag, is a perennial aquatic plant species belonging to the Cyperaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Mariscus tristis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat various conditions, including skin irritations, digestive issues, and respiratory infections.
